era_context: Late Early Dynastic Period in Mesopotamia; Fourth Dynasty Egypt; mature Indus Valley Civilization. This year falls within a period of relative stability in the Old World's major civilizations, though internal political dynamics in Mesopotamian city-states remain poorly documented.

events.title: Fourth Dynasty Egypt under Khafre or successor
events.description: The Fourth Dynasty continues its reign. Khafre (if he succeeded Khufu around -2500 BCE) would be consolidating his rule and continuing monumental construction, including the construction of his own pyramid complex at Giza. The exact chronology and regnal years remain uncertain. The centralized Egyptian state maintains control over the Nile Valley and engages in trade with the Levant and Nubia.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Khafre's pyramid complex and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Chronological uncertainty of ±10–15 years. The assignment of this year to Khafre's reign is based on conventional chronology but is not definitively established.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The historical record for this year is negligible. No contemporary written records document specific events. The record consists entirely of archaeological evidence and later compilations. The bias toward monumental and elite records means that the vast majority of human activity in this period is invisible to the historical record.
